title: PiFinder observing list (.pifinder)
description: Native versioned JSON observing-list format for PiFinder. The reference reader is lenient (it ignores unknown properties); this schema pins the recognized fields. See README.md and docs/adr/0016-pifinder-native-observing-list-format.md.

version.description: Format version. Readers reject anything but 1 with a PiFinderFormatError.

name.description: Display name of the observing list.

epoch.description: File-level default coordinate epoch (e.g. "J2000", "J2016.0"). Coordinate entries inherit this unless they carry their own 'epoch'. Non-J2000 coordinates are precessed to J2000 on read; PiFinder is J2000 throughout.

entry.description: An entry is either catalog-keyed or a coordinate entry. The presence of 'catalog_code' selects the kind.

catalogEntry.description: Catalog-keyed entry: resolved against the receiving device's catalog DB at load time. Only as portable as that DB.

sequence.description: Sequence within the catalog, e.g. 224 for NGC 224.

notes.description: Optional observing-list description for this target.

coordinateEntry.description: Self-contained entry carrying coordinates; used when an object is not catalog-keyed.

obj_type.description: Object type code (see README "Object type codes"), e.g. "Gx", "Nb", "*", "?".

ra.description: Right ascension in degrees, in the entry's effective epoch.

dec.description: Declination in degrees, in the entry's effective epoch.

epoch.description: Optional per-entry epoch override; defaults to the file-level 'epoch'.

magnitude.description: Either a bare number or a structured multi-band object.

mags.description: Raw magnitude values (numbers, or strings such as "~12").

filter_mag.description: Mean of the parseable magnitudes, used for filtering.

extents.description: Angular size / shape. Flat arrays are arcseconds; nested arrays are RA/Dec degrees and require 'geometry'.

shape.description: [d] = circle diameter; [major, minor] = ellipse; [v1, v2, ...] = radial polygon (all arcseconds). [[ra,dec], ...] = polyline vertices; [[[ra,dec],[ra,dec]], ...] = disconnected segments (degrees).
